latest.title: AGM Trading Update
latest.content:
15 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1874I
Mission Group PLC (The)
15 June 2026
15 June 2026
The Mission Group plc
("MISSION", the "Company" or "Group")
AGM Trading Update
Trading in line with expectations, strong Client retention and wins, continued focus on strategic delivery
Ahead of its Annual General Meeting held today,
MISSION
Group plc (AIM:TMG), a collective of sector-leading Creative, Sports and MarTech Agencies, provides the following update on trading for the period 1 January to 15 June 2026.
Trading in the current financial year remains in line with the Board's expectations. Against an ongoing backdrop of wider macroeconomic uncertainty this has been driven by continued strong Client retention, new Client wins and the benefits of the strategic actions completed earlier in the year to simplify and strengthen the Group's operating platform.
New Client wins were achieved across the Group's five practice areas: Integrated Marketing, Sports & Events, Property, PR and Healthcare. These included Westminster Council, Puma, Amaala Yacht Club, PwC, the International Tennis Federation and Volleyball World.
Good progress continues to be made against the key strategic priorities for 2026 outlined in the Group's full year results in March: US expansion execution, agency integration, consultancy offer launch, growth engine activation, capital allocation discipline and redefining the
MISSION
proposition. This includes further expansion of the pipeline of Client opportunities in the US within the Group's Mongoose Sports & Events practice. In addition, to further strengthen the combined capabilities of the Group's Integrated Marketing practice, Bray Leino was pleased to welcome globally recognised creative leader Wayne Deakin into the role of Chief Creative Officer.
A further update on trading will be provided, as usual, in a half year update in July.
ENDS
John Carey, Chief Executive
Giles Lee, Chief Financial Officer
Via Houston
The
MISSION
Group PLC
Simon Bridges/Andrew Potts/Harry Rees
Canaccord Genuity Limited
(Financial Adviser, Nominated Adviser and Broker)
020 7523 8000
Peter Tracey
Blackdown Partners Limited
(Financial Adviser)
020 3807 8484
Kate Hoare/Charlie Barker
Houston
(PR advisers)
077 3303 2695 / 0204 529 0549
E:
mission@houston.co.uk
NOTES TO EDITORS
MISSION Group is a collective of specialist agencies delivering marketing, communications and digital services for Clients across the UK and internationally. The Group combines deep sector expertise with connected capabilities across brand, digital, customer engagement, sponsorship, media, social, technology and communications.
Find out more at
www.themission.co.uk
Change of Accounting Reference Date
As previously announced, as a result of the change in the Group's accounting reference date, the Group's financial year will now end on 30 September annually, with its reporting timetable as follows:
· publication of audited annual financial statements for the 12-month period to 31 December 2025, by no later than 30 June 2026 (published on 4 June 2026);
· publication of unaudited interim accounts for the 6-month period to 30 June 2026, by no later than 30 September 2026;
· publication of audited annual financial statements for the 9-month period to 30 September 2026, by no later than 30 March 2027;
· publication of unaudited interim accounts for the 6-month period to 31 March 2027, by no later than 30 June 2027; and
· publication of audited annual financial statements for the 12-month period to 30 September 2027, by no later than 31 March 2028.
From then, annual and interim results will be published each year for the 12 months to 30 September and 6 months to 31 March, respectively.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.content:

RNS Number : 8945G
Mission Group PLC (The)
04 June 2026
4 June 2026
THE MISSION GROUP plc
("MISSION", "the Group")
Notice of AGM and Posting of Annual Report
The
MISSION
Group plc (AIM: TMG),
the Brand Performance Group, comprising of digital marketing and specialist communications Agencies
,
announces that copies of the Annual Report and Accounts in respect of the year ended 31 December 2025 have been sent to shareholders.
The Company also announces that Notice of the Annual General Meeting and the associated Proxy Form has been sent to shareholders. The Company's Annual General Meeting will be held on Monday 15 June 2026 at 12 noon at the offices of the Company, Fourth Floor, The Manufactory, 1-8 Alfred Mews, London W1T 7AA.
The Annual Report and the notice of AGM are available now on the Company's website at

The Mission Group plc
("MISSION" the "Company" or the "Group")
DIRECTOR/PDMR SHAREHOLDINGS
The
MISSION
Group plc (AIM: TMG) the Brand Performance Group, comprising of digital marketing and specialist communications Agencies,
has been notified of the following dealings in the Company's ordinary shares of 10p each ("
Ordinary Shares
"):
·
On 19 May 2026, David Zwirn, a person closely associated with Claudine Collins, Non-Executive Director, purchased 144,000 Ordinary Shares at a price of 13.99p per Ordinary Share.
·
On 20 May 2026, Jon Kempster, Non-Executive Director, purchased 68,852 Ordinary Shares at a price of 15.26p per Ordinary Share. His beneficial holding is 68,852 Ordinary Shares.
·
On 21 May 2026, Claudine Collins, Non-Executive Director, purchased 87,793 Ordinary Shares at a price of 17p per Ordinary Share. Her beneficial holding including those Ordinary Shares held by persons closely associated with her is 231,793 Ordinary Shares; and
·
On 21 May 2026, John Carey, Chief Executive Officer, purchased 175,587 Ordinary Shares at a price of 17p per Ordinary Share. His beneficial holding is 200,587 Ordinary Shares.

company_30s.what_they_do: TMG is a UK‑based marketing and communications group that sells brand‑management, digital media and public‑relations services to mid‑market and large corporates. They run a network of agencies that create, distribute and measure content across traditional and online channels.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The group sits at the intersection of advertising and data, a space that is expanding as brands shift budgets from TV to digital. Its network gives it a foothold in the £10‑plus‑billion UK marketing spend, and its client mix includes several long‑term contracts that can generate recurring revenue.
company_30s.current_state: The business is in a recovery phase – a new CEO was appointed last year, a growth‑share scheme was launched, and the board has been reshuffling. Cash burn remains a concern, but the company is now focusing on consolidating its agency portfolio and improving operating leverage. The share price is trading well below the valuation implied by the company’s recent earnings and the broader sector.

rns_narrative: The latest RNS releases paint a picture of a company in transition: a new CEO is steering a turnaround, a growth‑share scheme is designed to keep talent in place, and a series of board changes suggest a shift in strategy. The AGM results and trading update hint at a more disciplined cost structure, while the director‑shareholding reports show a steady decline in the stakes of former insiders.

There are two clear themes: 1) Management is actively restructuring the business to improve profitability, and 2) The company is signalling confidence in its future by launching a share‑buy‑back programme and a growth‑share plan. These actions could act as catalysts for a rebound once the market recognises the new direction.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silence until a turnaround is announced
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls are quietly hopeful that the CEO’s restructuring will unlock value. They point to the buy‑back and growth‑share scheme as signs that management believes the stock is undervalued.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears fear that the company’s cash burn and weak earnings still dominate. They worry that the new CEO may not deliver the turnaround quickly enough, and that the share‑buy‑back could simply be a cosmetic move.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from neutral to mildly bullish over the last two months, but overall engagement remains low. The community is still waiting for a concrete earnings beat or a clear operational milestone before taking a position.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent interest – the market is essentially unaware of the story, which is a perfect set‑up for a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The next AGM on 15 June will present the full year results and a detailed operating plan. A better‑than‑expected earnings beat or a clear path to profitability could trigger a jump.
The announced growth‑share scheme, if it is fully subscribed, will create a positive signal that the company is attracting talent and keeping key people motivated.
The share‑buy‑back programme, if it proceeds at the announced pace, will reduce supply and could lift the price once the market starts to see the buy‑back as real value creation.
A positive analyst upgrade or a new long‑term client contract would also act as a catalyst.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 5‑day volume spike that exceeds the 20‑day average, a break of the 12‑month moving average, or a clear earnings beat. Any of these would confirm that the market has started to price in the turnaround.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early stages of a deep crash‑recovery cycle. The APEX score suggests the price is compressed and ready to spring. A bounce will likely need a catalyst that demonstrates improved fundamentals, such as a better earnings report or a confirmed buy‑back.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company is deeply discounted – roughly 90% below its peak – yet the new CEO, buy‑back, and growth‑share scheme point to a serious attempt at restructuring. If the price recovers to the 50% of peak level (halfway to the ATH), the upside is about 400%. The risk is limited to a modest additional dip, which would represent only a few percent of the current price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the turnaround takes longer than the market expects, or that the company continues to burn cash without hitting profitability. In that case the stock could fall another 5–10% before stabilising, which would still leave a small cushion against the original peak.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside ≈ 400% versus a downside of ≈ 5% if the price falls further. The asymmetry is roughly 80:1 – a small loss for a very large potential gain.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the 12‑month moving average with volume 1.5× the 20‑day average. Allocate 10–15 % of the portfolio to this position, using a 1‑day stop just below the recent swing low. Target the 50% of peak level as a primary exit, with a trailing stop to lock in gains.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear earnings beat or a confirmed buy‑back announcement. A 5‑day volume spike and a 10‑day breakout would be a signal to enter. Keep a tight stop below the 20‑day low.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks. The only terminal risk is continued cash burn that forces a new capital raise, which would likely push the price lower.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A 90 % crash, a silent market and a new CEO’s restructuring give a contrarian trader a 400 % upside if the stock rebounds to half its peak, making this a high‑reward, low‑risk opportunity.
